title: Sha'a Gi
content:

{{Quote|This is the end. We're all doomed.|Sha'a Gi, during the Battle of Hypori|audio=ThisIsTheEnd-CWChapter20.flac|<ref name="CW20" />}}
'''Sha'a Gi''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Biniir/Legends|Ord Biniir]], as a [[Youngling/Legends|child]], his [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]] was discovered by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Daakman Barrek]]. Taking Gi as his Padawan, Barrek accompanied him to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] so that the youth could begin his [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. During his training, Gi was less than proficient in the martial skills of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Order]] but excelled in [[Computer/Legends|computer]] system operation. As a result, he aspired to work in the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] after passing his [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]]. Gi and his Master formed an effective partnership, gathering intelligence and disrupting [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] activity throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].

When the Clone Wars [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|broke out]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Gi and Barrek continued their intelligence-gathering operations and discovered a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Hypori droid factory|droid factory]] in operation on the planet [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]]. Barrek called in a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Task force/Legends|task force]] to destroy the factory, but the reinforcements were [[Battle of Hypori/Legends|ravaged]] by [[Space mine/Legends|orbital mines]], and the entire force crashed onto the surface of the planet. Gi and Barrek regrouped with several Jedi survivors. Surrounded by [[Droid/Legends|droid]] forces, Barrek was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the leader of the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]], a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] called [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. As the remaining Jedi took cover in the wreck of a downed [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]] and waited for Grievous to engage them in battle, Gi—overcome by fear and the loss of his Master—panicked and rushed out into the open, where he was killed by Grievous.

==Biography==
===Early life===
Sha'a Gi, a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was native to [[Ord Biniir/Legends|Ord Biniir]],<ref name="DB-Sha'a Gi" /> a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Relgim sector/Legends|Relgim sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA-OC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> As a [[Youngling/Legends|child]], his connection with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] was discovered by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Daakman Barrek]]. Taking the youth as his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], Barrek escorted him to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] so that Gi could be [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].<ref name="DB-Sha'a Gi" />

[[File:BarrekHolo-CW20.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Sha'a Gi's Master, Daakman Barrek]]
During his training, it was evident that Gi's combat-related skills were not on par with those of his contemporaries, but he did display remarkable aptitude with [[Computer/Legends|computer]] systems. As a result, Gi hoped to take up a position in the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] once he had completed his [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]]. Gi struggled with maintaining the mental discipline required for members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], and had trouble controlling his fear on occasions where he was required to work independently. The presence of his Master often acted as a stabilizing influence, however, and the pair formed an effective team. Gi and Barrek were often deployed to the Outer Rim Territories to gather intelligence on [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] activity and then counter the threat that it posed.<ref name="DB-Sha'a Gi" />

===Participating in the Clone Wars===
In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted across the galaxy as the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] took up arms against the ruling [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="AotC">{{Film|II}}</ref> Gi and Barrek were on a mission disrupting pirate activity in the Outer Rim when word came through that Barrek's [[Daakman Barrek's first Padawan|first Padawan]] had been [[Death/Legends|killed]] during the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]]. Barrek accepted the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] within the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] and dedicated himself to the war effort. As his Padawan, Gi followed his Master's lead.<ref name="DB-Barrek">{{DB|character|daakmanbarrek|Barrek, Daakman}}</ref>

[[File:Tarr-Mundi-Gi-CW20.png|thumb|right|250px|Tarr Seirr, Ki-Adi-Mundi and Sha'a Gi prepare to face Grievous on Hypori.]]
Gi and Barrek continued their intelligence-gathering operations in the Outer Rim Territories, and, shortly after the outbreak of the war, the pair discovered a [[Hypori droid factory|droid foundry]] operated by the Confederacy on the planet [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]]<ref name="DB-Sha'a Gi" /> in the [[Ferra sector/Legends|Ferra sector]].<ref name="TEA-OC" /> Gi and Barrek reported their discovery, and the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] dispatched a force of Jedi and [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] to the planet. The arrival of the [[Task force/Legends|task force]] was countered by the Confederacy, which used [[Space mine/Legends|orbital mines]] to [[Battle of Hypori/Legends|destroy]] all of the incoming [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Starship/Legends|craft]], causing them to crash behind enemy lines. Most of the [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Soldier/Legends|troopers]] were killed during the crash landings, and the survivors were cut down by [[Droid/Legends|droid]] weapons fire. Gi and Barrek rendezvoused with the Jedi assigned to the mission—Masters [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]], [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]], and [[K'Kruhk]], and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]] [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]] and [[Tarr Seirr]]. The Jedi took refuge in the wreck of an [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]] and were surrounded by droid forces under the command of the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], the [[Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the Confederacy's [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]].<ref name="DB-Barrek" />

===Death===
{{Quote|It's all around us!|Sha'a Gi, referring to Grievous's footsteps|<ref name="CW20" />}}
[[File:GrievousRevealed.jpg|thumb|left|General Grievous killed Sha'a Gi by landing on him from above.]]
During the fierce fighting, Master Barrek managed to contact Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who had recently [[Battle of Muunilinst/Legends|taken]] the planet [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]] for the Republic. Barrek informed his fellow General of the situation on Hypori before he was killed by Grievous. Without Master Barrek's presence, Gi began to lose his nerve as his self-doubt and fear started to overcome him. From inside the wreck, the Jedi were able to hear Grievous approaching, and Gi was visibly unnerved by the sound and the fact that they were unable to see the cyborg general. As the assembled Jedi waited for Grievous to assault them, Gi panicked and rushed out of the wreckage into the open. Grievous appeared from above Gi, landing on the young Padawan.<ref name="CW20" /> Grievous' talons speared the young Jedi, and he was crushed to death by the general's weight.<ref name="DB-Sha'a Gi" /> Grievous went on to battle the assembled Jedi, killing Tarr Seirr and wounding the others.<ref name="CW20" /> The survivors were eventually rescued by [[the Muunilinst 10]], an elite [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC troopers]] that had been dispatched from Muunilinst.<ref name="CW21">{{CW|21}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|We can't face him!|Sha'a Gi, to Ki-Adi Mundi, referring to Grievous|<ref name="CW20" />}}
[[File:ShaaGiScreaming-CWChapter20.jpg|thumb|right|Sha'a Gi succumbs to his fear.]]
Sha'a Gi was a Human male with brown-[[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and light-colored skin.<ref name="CW20" /> He stood at 1.85 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. Awkward and full of self-doubt, Gi found that maintaining the mental discipline required by Jedi was hard for him. He had difficulty controlling his fear, especially when working independently. Nevertheless, Master Barrek's presence was enough to calm the young man, and the pair worked effectively together. When Barrek was killed by General Grievous on Hypori, Gi allowed his fear to overwhelm him, which led to his death at the cyborg General's hands.<ref name="DB-Sha'a Gi" />

==Powers and abilities==
Sha'a Gi was a young Padawan being trained to Knighthood. He was trained to use a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], despite having less proficiency in the martial skills and combat arts than other Padawans at his level.<ref name="DB-Sha'a Gi" />

==Equipment==
Sha'a Gi wielded a green-bladed lightsaber and wore [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]]. As a Padawan, Gi still wore the traditional [[Padawan braid/Legends|braid]] in his hair.<ref name="DB-Sha'a Gi" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:JohnDiMaggioIMDB.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Sha'a Gi was voiced by John DiMaggio.]]
In the audio commentary included on the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]] DVD, director [[Genndy Tartakovsky]] states that the Human Jedi featured in Chapter 20 was inspired by the character [[Wikipedia:Shaggy Rogers|Shaggy Rogers]] from the [[Wikipedia:Scooby-Doo|''Scooby-Doo'']] cartoons. This resulted in a similar appearance to that character. Initially, Tartakovsy wanted the Jedi to have more of a high-pitched voice like Shaggy. However, Tartakovsky realized that the voice didn't fit and became "too goofy." The character was listed simply as "Padawan" in the end credits of the ''Clone Wars'' Volume One DVD,<ref name="CW Vol I">[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]]</ref> but soon received the name "Sha'a Gi" when [[StarWars.com]] added the character to its [[Databank (original)|Databank]].<ref name="DB-Sha'a Gi" /> Sha'a Gi was voiced in his single appearance in the ''Clone Wars'' cartoon by [[John DiMaggio]].<ref name="CW Vol I" />

Gi made an appearance as a "hero unit" defending Hypori for the Galactic Republic in the turn-based strategy game ''[[Star Wars: Battle for the Republic]]'' released by [[THQ]] Wireless for [[Wikipedia:Cell phone|cell phones]]<ref name="BRepublic">''[[Star Wars: Battle for the Republic]]''</ref> in [[November]] [[2005]].<ref name="BRepublic-Date">{{WebCite|url=https://www.ign.com/games/star-wars-battle-for-the-republic|text=Star Wars: Battle for the Republic|work=IGN|archivedate=20200420054056}}</ref>
